chain.thought:
Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
the total number of playoffs appearances -> playoffs appearances
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
all teams -> team

The question is asking for the sum of all playoffs appearances across all teams listed. Since each row represents a team and the "playoffs appearances" column indicates how many times each team has appeared in the playoffs, we need to aggregate the values in the "playoffs appearances" column to get the total. We can use the f_sum(playoffs appearances) operation to calculate the total number of playoffs appearances.

To find the total number of playoffs appearances from all teams, we need to add up all the appearances listed in the table. 

Adding the appearances:  
1 (Anderson Packers) + 0 (BAA Buffalo) + 0 (BAA Indianapolis) + 3 (Baltimore Bullets*) + 4 (Chicago Stags) + 1 (Cleveland Rebels) + 0 (Denver Nuggets) + 0 (Detroit Falcons) + 0 (Indianapolis Jets) + 4 (Indianapolis Olympians) + 0 (Pittsburgh Ironmen) + 0 (Providence Steamrollers) + 1 (Sheboygan Red Skins) + 3 (St. Louis Bombers) + 0 (Toronto Huskies) + 4 (Washington Capitols) + 0 (Waterloo Hawks) = 21  

Therefore, the total number of playoffs appearances from all teams is 21.
